---

commit 996e01f758141a7930faa338eb4b1387d59fe5ab
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sun May 3 02:41:43 2026 -0400

    feat(agent): operator-cancel via bead-store marker + mid-attempt poll [ddx-2e54b560]
    
    ADR-022 step 3. POST /api/beads/<id>/cancel writes extra.cancel-requested:true
    on the bead store. The execute-bead worker polls the marker every
    CancelPollInterval (10s default) while an attempt is in flight; on the first
    positive read it cancels the dispatch context (killing the agent subprocess
    at the next safe point), writes cancel-honored:true, and emits a
    preserved_for_review result with reason "operator_cancel". Subsequent cancels
    are silent no-ops once cancel-honored is set.

 cli/internal/bead/store.go                     |  65 ++++++
 cli/internal/server/server.go                  |  27 +++
 cli/internal/server/server_cancel_test.go      |  92 ++++++++
 cli/internal/server/workers.go                 |  14 +-
 8 files changed, 601 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

200		<-startedDispatch
201		<-runner.startedAt
202		cancelStore.request()
203		cancelAt := time.Now()
204	
205		select {
206		case res := <-done:
207			latency := time.Since(cancelAt)
208			// Allow up to 4× the poll interval to absorb scheduler + worktree
209			// teardown overhead. The SLA contract is "within one poll interval at
210			// the next safe point"; this test asserts the bound is honored, not
211			// the wall-clock minimum.
212			if latency > 4*pollInterval+250*time.Millisecond {
213				t.Errorf("cancel latency %v exceeded SLA budget (%v)", latency, 4*pollInterval+250*time.Millisecond)
214			}
215			if res == nil || res.Reason != OperatorCancelReason {
216				t.Errorf("expected operator_cancel result, got %+v", res)
217			}
218		case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
219			t.Fatal("cancel did not propagate within 5s")
220		}
221	}
222	
223	// TestOperatorCancel_DuringRealClaudeAttempt is the WIRED-IN integration test
224	// (AC#7). Instead of swapping in a stub for dispatchAgentRun, it exercises
225	// the real end-to-end path: a long-running script-style runner that polls the
226	// caller's context (the same contract a real claude subprocess satisfies via
227	// SIGTERM on cancel) is dispatched by ExecuteBeadWithConfig; the operator
228	// cancel marker is flipped on a real *bead.Store via RequestCancel; the
229	// worker must observe the marker through its mid-attempt poll, propagate
230	// cancellation into the in-flight dispatch (proving the subprocess receives
231	// the cancel signal), and emit preserved_for_review/operator_cancel.
232	//
233	// This is not a stub-attempt cancel — RequestCancel writes to the same bead
234	// store the worker reads, and the runner only returns when its dispatch ctx
235	// is cancelled by the poll loop, demonstrating the cancel actually traveled
236	// through the live signal path.
237	func TestOperatorCancel_DuringRealClaudeAttempt(t *testing.T) {
238		const beadID = "ddx-cancel-realattempt"
239	
240		prev := CancelPollInterval
241		CancelPollInterval = 25 * time.Millisecond
242		t.Cleanup(func() { CancelPollInterval = prev })
243	
244		projectRoot := setupArtifactTestProjectRoot(t)
245		gitOps := &artifactTestGitOps{
246			projectRoot: projectRoot,
247			baseRev:     "real000000000001",
248			resultRev:   "real000000000001",
249			wtSetupFn: func(wtPath string) {
250				setupArtifactTestWorktree(t, wtPath, beadID, "", false, 0)
251			},
252		}
253	
254		// Use the real *bead.Store rooted at the project's .ddx, the same one
255		// the server's handleCancelBead writes to via beadStoreForRequest.
256		beadStore := newCancelTestBeadStore(t, projectRoot, beadID)
257	
258		// A runner that blocks like a real claude subprocess until its context
259		// is signalled. Exit code 1 + ctx.Err() mirrors what the OS executor
260		// returns when SIGTERM kills the child mid-stream.
261		runner := newBlockingAgentRunner()
262	
263		// The "operator" hits POST /api/beads/<id>/cancel — modeled here by
264		// calling RequestCancel directly, the function the HTTP handler calls.
265		subprocessSawCancel := make(chan struct{})
266		go func() {
267			<-runner.startedAt
268			// Wait one extra tick to make sure the worker's poll loop is the
269			// one that drives the abort, not a context already-cancelled state.
270			time.Sleep(2 * CancelPollInterval)
271			if _, err := beadStore.RequestCancel(beadID); err != nil {
272				t.Errorf("RequestCancel: %v", err)
273			}
274			close(subprocessSawCancel)
275		}()
276	
277		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
278	
279		done := make(chan *ExecuteBeadResult, 1)
280		go func() {
281			res, _ := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
282				AgentRunner: runner,
283				BeadCancel:  beadStore,
284			}, gitOps)
285			done <- res
286		}()
287	
288		<-subprocessSawCancel
289	
290		var res *ExecuteBeadResult
291		select {
292		case res = <-done:
293		case <-time.After(10 * time.Second):
294			t.Fatal("worker did not honor cancel on a real attempt within 10s")
295		}
296	
297		if res == nil || res.Reason != OperatorCancelReason {
298			t.Fatalf("expected preserved/operator_cancel, got %+v", res)
299		}
300		if res.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview {
301			t.Errorf("Status = %q, want %q", res.Status, ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview)
302		}
303	
304		// And the cancel-honored marker must be persisted on the real bead.
305		requested, err := beadStore.IsCancelRequested(beadID)
306		if err != nil {
307			t.Fatalf("IsCancelRequested: %v", err)
308		}
309		if requested {
310			t.Error("IsCancelRequested still true after worker honored — cancel-honored marker missing")
311		}
312	}

data.text:
The bead's implementation already landed on `main` via earlier merge `48684e63` (commit `996e01f7`), which is part of base-rev `9b17c6e7`. All four required tests exist and pass:
- `TestOperatorCancel_SetsBeadExtra` (server_cancel_test.go:14)
- `TestWorker_HonorsCancelMidAttempt` (execute_bead_cancel_test.go:99)
- `TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA` (execute_bead_cancel_test.go:166)
- `TestOperatorCancel_DuringRealClaudeAttempt` (execute_bead_cancel_test.go:237)

The prior BLOCK note observed only `result.json` in that attempt's diff because the source had already merged on a previous attempt; this is a re-attempt against a base that already contains the work. Recording a `no_changes_rationale.txt` with a verification command, per the no_changes contract.

input.content:
no_changes: implementation for ADR-022 step 3 (operator-cancel via bead-store
marker + mid-attempt poll) is already present in the repository at base-rev
9b17c6e7. It landed via merge 48684e63 of feature commit 996e01f7
("feat(agent): operator-cancel via bead-store marker + mid-attempt poll
[ddx-2e54b560]"). The prior REVIEW:BLOCK observed only an execution-evidence
result.json in that attempt's diff because the source code had already merged
on the preceding attempt; nothing more was left to commit.

Acceptance criteria mapped to evidence in the current worktree:

  AC1 — POST /api/beads/<id>/cancel writes extra.cancel-requested:true:
        cli/internal/server/server.go (handleCancelBead);
        cli/internal/bead/store.go (RequestCancel).

  AC2 — Worker mid-attempt loop polls bead extra every 10s:
        cli/internal/agent/cancel_poll.go (CancelPollInterval = 10s,
        startCancelPoller); wired in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go.

  AC3 — On cancel-requested, worker emits preserved_for_review with reason
        "operator_cancel"; cancel-honored:true is set:
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go (OperatorCancelReason,
        ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview); poll loop in
        cli/internal/agent/cancel_poll.go writes cancel-honored via
        bead.Store.HonorCancel.

  AC4 — Idempotent (second cancel is silent no-op once cancel-honored is set):
        cli/internal/bead/store.go (RequestCancel short-circuits when
        cancel-honored is already true).

  AC5 — Tests TestOperatorCancel_SetsBeadExtra,
        TestWorker_HonorsCancelMidAttempt, TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA:
        cli/internal/server/server_cancel_test.go:14;
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_cancel_test.go:99 and :166.

  AC6 — go test green; lefthook pre-commit passes (verification command
        below re-runs the cancel-related tests).

  AC7 — WIRED-IN integration test TestOperatorCancel_DuringRealClaudeAttempt
        uses a real *bead.Store + RequestCancel and a blocking runner that
        only returns when its dispatch context is cancelled — proving the
        cancel travels the live signal path, not a stub:
        cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_cancel_test.go:237.

The only working-tree delta vs base-rev is an unrelated deletion of
.agents/skills/sample-skill, which is outside this bead's scope and is
intentionally left unstaged.

verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/ ./internal/server/ -run 'TestOperatorCancel_SetsBeadExtra|TestWorker_HonorsCancelMidAttempt|TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA|TestOperatorCancel_DuringRealClaudeAttempt' -count=1 -timeout 90s

data.text: All four required tests pass against the current base-rev. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` with a `verification_command` that re-runs `TestOperatorCancel_SetsBeadExtra`, `TestWorker_HonorsCancelMidAttempt`, `TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA`, and the WIRED-IN `TestOperatorCancel_DuringRealClaudeAttempt`. Implementation already merged via `996e01f7` / `48684e63`; no new commit needed.
